---
title: "Monte Carlo Integration: Importance and Rejection Sampling"
author: "PhD Applicant"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

## Overview

This document demonstrates Monte Carlo Integration and Rejection Sampling. These methods are used to compute normalizing constants and draw samples from complex probability density functions where analytical integration is not feasible.

**Target Function:**
$f(x) = k \cdot \exp(-x^2/2)(\sin((4x)^2) + 1)$, for $x \in [0, 5]$.

## Algorithm Implementation

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
set.seed(42)
```

```{r mc-functions}
# Unnormalized target PDF
unnorm_pdf <- function(x) {
  val <- exp(-x^2 / 2) * (sin((4*x)^2) + 1)
  # Return 0 if outside [0, 5]
  ifelse(x >= 0 & x <= 5, val, 0)
}

importance_sampling <- function(N = 10000) {
  # 1. Sample from proposal q(x) ~ Log-Normal(0,1)
  x_samples <- rlnorm(N, meanlog = 0, sdlog = 1)
  
  # 2. Evaluate q(x)
  q_x <- dlnorm(x_samples, meanlog = 0, sdlog = 1)
  
  # 3. Compute weights
  f_x <- unnorm_pdf(x_samples)
  weights <- ifelse(q_x > 0, f_x / q_x, 0)
  
  # 4. Estimate integral
  integral_estimate <- mean(weights)
  k_estimate <- 1.0 / integral_estimate
  
  return(c(integral = integral_estimate, k = k_estimate))
}

rejection_sampling <- function(N = 10000) {
  # Proposal distribution g(x) ~ Exp(1)
  g <- function(x) dexp(x, rate = 1)
  
  # Find optimal scaling constant C using optimization
  # Maximize f(x)/g(x) in the interval [0, 5]
  opt_res <- optimize(f = function(x) unnorm_pdf(x) / g(x), 
                      interval = c(0, 5), maximum = TRUE)
  C <- opt_res$objective
  
  cat(sprintf("Optimal scaling constant C: %.4f\n", C))
  
  samples <- numeric(N)
  count <- 0
  attempts <- 0
  
  while(count < N) {
    attempts <- attempts + 1
    x_prop <- rexp(1, rate = 1)
    u <- runif(1, min = 0, max = C * g(x_prop))
    
    if (u <= unnorm_pdf(x_prop)) {
      count <- count + 1
      samples[count] <- x_prop
    }
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("Acceptance Rate: %.2f%%\n", (N / attempts) * 100))
  return(samples)
}
```

## Example Usage

```{r run-mc}
# Run Importance Sampling
is_results <- importance_sampling(N = 100000)
cat(sprintf("Importance Sampling -> Estimated Integral: %.4f\n", is_results["integral"]))
cat(sprintf("Importance Sampling -> Normalizing Constant k: %.4f\n", is_results["k"]))

# Run Rejection Sampling
cat("\nStarting Rejection Sampling...\n")
rej_samples <- rejection_sampling(N = 5000)
cat("Successfully drew 5000 samples.\n")
```
